rulings.Case Title: MARCIA GIUSTI et al VS. BKCM ENTERPRISES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORAION et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 14, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTS JOHN SIMONSE, MAGNATE FUND #2 LLC, MAGNATE FUND #3 LLC, JWS CAPITAL INVESTMENTS, 27TH STREET ASSOCIATES LLC, 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, SOUTH VAN NESS ASSOCIATES LLC, LHJS INVESTMENTS, LLC, 55 WOODWARD, LLC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ROY POOLE VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL FOUNDATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 14, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The Bietz declaration, the objections to which are overruled, raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether defendant's staff met the standard of care. The motion for summary adjudication as to the second cause of action for battery is granted. Plaintiff has conceded that restraining him does not constitute a battery by virtue of implied consent in the treatment of an emergency. Rather, plaintiff complains of the manner and duration of the restraint. That raises issues of negligence, but does not constitute such a different procedure as to support a claim for medical battery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: PETER BOWES VS. ANNABELLES BAR AND BISTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Written Disco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 14, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PETER BOWES' Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel is denied. The moving party has failed to comply with the Order of this Court dated, June 30, 2014, which continued the motion to today conditioned on delivery of courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302 on or before June 30, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MANDIP DHILLON VS. LASERAWAY MEDICAL GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Mandip Dhillon'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Re Plaintiff'S Employment History And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 14, 2014, Line 3, Plaintiff Mandip Dhillon'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Re Plaintiff'S Employment History And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to quash is granted. The defendant has failed to meet the necessary enhanced burden to show relevance applicable to employment records from previous employers. See Board of Trustees v. Superior Court (1981) 119 Cal. App. 3d 516 [174 Cal. Rptr. 160]. Sanctions in the sum of $1,000.00 are awarded as against defendant, to be paid within thirty days of the date of this Order . Sanction are awarded because of defendant's failure to engage in a meaningful "meet and confer" process after receipt of plaintiff's "meet and confer" letter which should have resulted in a significant limitation as to the scope of the subpoenas. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RUSLAN VORONETSKY VS. CAL WOOD FLOORING SUPPLY, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 14, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT CAL WOOD FLOORING SUPPLY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately alleges that Plaintiff's injuries limited major life activities and therefore fall under the FEHA definition of "disability." (SAC, pars. 20-22; Gov. Code sec. 12926.) The SAC adequately alleges that Defendant was aware of Plaintiff's disability, but Defendant took no affirmative steps to accommodate him. (SAC, par. 21.) Cal. Code of Regulations, Tit. 2 sec. 11068 provides that an employer has an affirmative duty to make reasonable accommodations for an employee's disability. The SAC adequately alleges that the harassment went beyond the scope of conduct necessary for job performance. (SAC, pars. 23-25, 51.) The SAC adequately alleges the causation element of the retaliation cause of action. (SAC, par. 27.) The SAC adequately states a cause of action for violation of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200 et seq. (See Herr v. Nestle USA, Inc. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 779, 789.) The claims remaining in the SAC are not preempted by the Worker's Compensation Act. Disability discrimination claims and wrongful discharge claims arising from disability discrimination are not preempted. (City of Moorpark v. Sup. Ct. (1998) 18 Cal.4th 1143, 1154-55.) Claims based on failure to accommodate a worker's disability are also not preempted. (Bagatti v. Dept. of Rehabilitation (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 344, 364-68.) Plaintiff's counsel is cautioned to comply with CRC 3.1113(f) in all future filings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)
